D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Iridescent Begonia — Photonic Chloroplasts

Begonia pavonina has iridoplasts — modified chloroplasts with stacked thylakoid lamellae acting as a photonic crystal — they reflect blue while still doing photosynthesis under deep-shade conditions

Jacobs 2016 showed Begonia pavonina leaves have specialised "iridoplasts" — chloroplasts whose stacked thylakoid lamellae form a 1D photonic crystal reflecting blue. The structure simultaneously improves green/red light capture for shade-grown photosynthesis. Trade-off between reflection (blue, visible iridescence) and capture (longer wavelengths).

Toggle on/off the iridoplast structure to compare leaf appearance.
